verdicts.new_quote:
singleton.py:1-5 (unchanged verbatim): """Singleton server management for multi-session environments.

When multiple Claude Code sessions start simultaneously, this module ensures
only ONE HTTP server process is spawned. All sessions connect to it as
lightweight stdio proxies. || cli.py:110-121: root = server._default_session_root() ... print(f"session root: {root}  (exists: {root.exists()})") ... print(f"session cap : {_human(server._session_storage_cap_bytes())}  [STEALTH_MCP_SESSION_STORAGE_CAP_GB]") || proxy_forwarder.py:1: """Authenticated proxy forwarding for browser launch proxies.""" || proxy_utils.py:1: """Proxy parsing and Chrome launch-arg helpers."""
verdicts.current_location: src/stealth_chrome_devtools_mcp/embedded/singleton.py:1-5 + cli.py:110-121 (also: cli.py:202 _cmd_doctor's own "session root" print, embedded/process_cleanup.py:350-370, embedded/proxy_forwarder.py:1, embedded/proxy_utils.py:1)
verdicts.evidence: Read all five files at HEAD (c7a2952b). singleton.py's docstring is byte-for-byte unchanged ("multi-session environments" / "lightweight stdio proxies"). cli.py's _cmd_status (now lines 105-122, was ~95) and _cmd_doctor (lines 192-210) both still print 'session root' and 'session cap' referring purely to the profile-clone disk-cap bookkeeping, with zero cue distinguishing this from an MCP session. process_cleanup.py still overloads 'session' for process-lifecycle scoping (line 370: 'predates this session', mildly reworded from the original quoted 'pre-date this server session' but semantically identical). proxy_forwarder.py and proxy_utils.py docstrings are unchanged, so the unrelated 'proxy' (egress HTTP/SOCKS) vs 'proxy' (stdio-to-HTTP bridge in singleton.py) collision persists. This is a naming/glossary issue; none of the quality-gates changes (ruff format, settings.py, noqa tags, README additions) touch terminology, so nothing here was addressed.

verdicts.new_quote:
dynamic_hook_system.py:378-380: async def _process_request_hooks(self, tab, request: RequestInfo, event=None):  # noqa: C901,PLR0912,PLR0915  DEBT(F-165)
        """Process hooks for a request/response in real-time with priority chain
        processing.""" ... line 395: matching_hooks.sort(key=lambda h: h.priority) ... line 445: hook = matching_hooks[0]
verdicts.current_location: src/stealth_chrome_devtools_mcp/embedded/dynamic_hook_system.py:378-380 (docstring), 395 (sort), 445 (matching_hooks[0]); also embedded/hook_learning_system.py:512 ("Use priority (lower = higher priority) to control [execution order]")
verdicts.evidence: Function moved from ~line 269 to line 378 (earlier code inserted upstream) and gained a ruff-wrapped docstring plus a `# noqa: C901,PLR0912,PLR0915  DEBT(F-165)` complexity-debt tag, but the logic is identical: matching_hooks is sorted by priority then only `matching_hooks[0]` is ever passed to _execute_hook_action — every lower-priority hook on an overlapping pattern still silently never fires. The misleading 'priority chain processing' docstring and hook_learning_system.py's 'controls execution order' claim (moved from line 465 to 512) are both still present verbatim, so the doc/behavior mismatch the finding describes is untouched. The added noqa tag acknowledges cyclomatic complexity, not this semantic issue, and Stage-3 fix plans for this were confirmed not yet executed.

matches() moved from line 119 to line 115-165 (ruff re-wrapped the dict literal and eval call across more lines) and the eval() call now carries `# noqa: S307  PERMANENT(eval hook matching by design)`, an owner-tagged acknowledgment that eval() itself is intentional. But that tag only silences the Bandit/ruff security lint about eval-on-arbitrary-string; it does nothing about the finding's actual complaint, which is the missing compile()-once caching. The condition source string is still re-parsed and re-evaluated from scratch via bare eval() on every single matching request, with no compiled-code-object cache anywhere in the class. Behaviorally and architecturally unchanged.

verdicts.current_location: src/stealth_chrome_devtools_mcp/embedded/element_cloner.py:18 (class defs shifted a couple lines each from ruff formatting; combined size now ~2686 lines, up from ~2223, purely from reformatting/wrapping, not new functionality)
verdicts.evidence: All five cloner classes still exist as fully separate implementations; none were consolidated. Directly confirmed the mirrored method-family pattern still holds: ElementCloner defines extract_element_styles/_structure/_events/_animations/_assets/clone_element_complete, while FileBasedElementCloner independently re-defines extract_element_styles_to_file/_structure_to_file/..._to_file/clone_element_complete_to_file — the exact parallel duplication the finding describes. This is an architectural/structural finding; the quality-gates wave (formatting, settings.py, noqa tags) does not touch design-level duplication, and the cloner-duplication fix plan was confirmed not executed.

verdicts.new_quote:
def _cmd_status(_args) -> int:
    server = _server()
    import singleton

    port = singleton._find_running_server()
    root = server._default_session_root()
    print(f"backend     : {'running on port ' + str(port) if port else 'not running'}")
    print(f"version     : {singleton._server_version()}")
    print(f"session root: {root}  (exists: {root.exists()})")
verdicts.evidence: Both _cmd_status and _cmd_doctor were reformatted by ruff (blank-line spacing, multi-line f-strings for the _human() calls) and shifted down ~10 lines, but the logic is identical: both call singleton._find_running_server(), which returns only the port, and neither ever reads or prints state['pid'] from ~/.stealth-mcp/server.json. No PID is exposed in either command's output. The settings.py/pydantic-settings migration and observability.py addition did not touch this code path.

verdicts.new_quote:
## CLI

Installs a `stealth-chrome-devtools` ops command for managing the server and its
disk usage. (This is for *ops* — to drive a browser, use the MCP server or its
HTTP backend.)

```bash
stealth-chrome-devtools status       # backend running? session root + caps
stealth-chrome-devtools profiles     # list profiles with size / role / in-use
stealth-chrome-devtools cleanup      # preview reclaimable disk (DRY RUN)
stealth-chrome-devtools cleanup --apply               # actually reclaim
stealth-chrome-devtools cleanup --session-cap-gb 12   # preview at a tighter cap
stealth-chrome-devtools doctor       # check Chrome / environment
stealth-chrome-devtools serve --http --port 19222     # start the server
```
verdicts.current_location: README.md:214-228 (identical line range and byte-for-byte identical text to the original finding)
verdicts.evidence: The '## CLI' section sits at the exact same lines (214-228) with content unchanged verbatim from the original quote — no `uv run` prefix was added to any example, and no clarifying note was inserted. The '### Local Development' section (line 64) still sets up the uv-driven mental model (mcpServers config invoking `uv --directory ... run stealth-chrome-devtools-mcp`), and `uv run` still appears only in the unrelated Testing section (lines 184-190, pytest commands). The gates wave added a new '## Development setup' section near the bottom of the file (line 264) and an '## Error Reporting (opt-in)' section (line 248) for Sentry, but neither touches or cross-references the CLI section, so the exact same-file inconsistency the finding describes is fully intact.
